functions.name: getInformationFromMemory
functions.description: Retrieve historical conversational context through a semantic search. Number of tokens formula: num_chunks x (num_neighbour_chunks+1) x 256 (chunk size). Example: {"name": "getInformationFromMemory", "parameters": {"conversationID": "123456", "query": "What was discussed earlier?", "num_chunks": 5, "num_neighbour_chunks": 2, "similarity_threshold": 0.8}}

num_chunks.description: How many chunks are considered semantically. More chunks for bigger context.

num_neighbour_chunks.description: Add neighbour chunk to every semantically considered chunk. Higher value for general queries or wider range of answers.

similarity_threshold.description: Threshold for resulting chunks. Higher threshold for fewer but more precise answers. Usually there is no need to go higher than 0.9.

functions.name: getInformationFromUser
functions.description: Ask for more information. Example: {"name": "getInformationFromUser", "parameters": {"headline": "About Your Pets", "questions": [{"question": "What's your pet's name?", "input": {"placeholder": "Pet's name"}}, {"question": "What type of pet do you have?", "radio": {"labels": ["Dog", "Cat", "Bird", "Other"]}}, {"question": "When did you get your pet?", "date": {}}, {"question": "Does your pet have any special needs?", "checkbox": {"labels": ["Special Diet", "Medication", "Accessibility Needs"]}}]}}

functions.name: getExternalFunctions
functions.description: Retrieve available external functions AiDA can perform through a semantic lookup using an action description. The action description should denote a generalized action AiDA might perform, not the user's specific request. The function suggests actions suitable for chaining. Actions should be comma-separated and the categories should be ordered in sync with the actions in the comma-separated list. If no function is returned, it implies there's no available external function for the described action. Example action descriptions: 'web search', 'semantic analysis', 'web search,semantic analysis', 'retrieve financial data'.

num_results.description: The number of desired results. More results for broader search.

similarity_threshold.description: Threshold for resulting functions. Higher threshold for fewer but more precise results. Usually there is no need to go higher than 0.9.

category.description: The category of function to be performed. You can provide multiple categories, separated by commas, in sync with the order of the actions. E.g., 'Information Retrieval,Data Processing'.

informationretrieval_functions.name: general
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a regular Google search. Example: {"name": "general", "parameters": {"q": "OpenAI"}}

q.description: Defines the query you want to search. You can use anything that you would use in a regular Google search. Supports advanced search query parameters. See the full list of supported advanced search query parameters at https://serpapi.com/advanced-google-query-parameters.

location.description: Defines from where you want the search to originate. If several locations match the location requested, the most popular one is picked. Use https://serpapi.com/locations-api for more precise control.

start.description: Defines the result offset. It skips the given number of results. It's used for pagination. Google Local Results only accepts multiples of 20 as the start value.

num.description: Defines the maximum number of results to return.

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: maps
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a Google Maps search. Example: {"name": "maps", "parameters": {"q": "restaurants near me"}}

q.description: Defines the query you want to search. You can use anything that you would use in a regular Google Maps search. The parameter is only required if type is set to search.

ll.description: Defines GPS coordinates of location where you want your q (query) to be applied. It has to be constructed in the next sequence: @ + latitude + , + longitude + , + zoom. This will form a string that looks like this: e.g. @40.7455096,-74.0083012,14z. The zoom parameter is optional but recommended for higher precision (it ranges from 3z, map completely zoomed out - to 21z, map completely zoomed in). The parameter should only be used when type is set to search.

data.description: Parameter is only required if type is set to place. In that case, it defines a search for a specific place. It has to be constructed in the next sequence: !4m5!3m4!1s + data_id + !8m2!3d + latitude + !4d + longitude. This will form a string that looks like this: e.g. !4m5!3m4!1s0x89c259b7abdd4769:0xc385876db174521a!8m2!3d40.750231!4d-74.004019. Parameter can also be used to filter the search results.

place_id.description: Defines the unique reference to a place on a Google Map. Place IDs are available for most locations, including businesses, landmarks, parks, and intersections.

type.description: Defines the type of search you want to make. It can be set to: search - returns a list of results for the set q parameter, place - returns results for a specific place when data parameter is set

start.description: Defines the result offset. It skips the given number of results. It's used for pagination. (e.g., 0 (default) is the first page of results, 20 is the 2nd page of results, 40 is the 3rd page of results, etc.).

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: jobs
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a job search. Example: {"name": "jobs", "parameters": {"q": "software engineer"}}

q.description: Defines the query you want to search.

location.description: Defines from where you want the search to originate. If several locations match the location requested, the most popular one is picked. Use https://serpapi.com/locations-api for more precise control.

start.description: Defines the result offset. It skips the given number of results. It's used for pagination. (e.g., 0 (default) is the first page of results, 10 is the 2nd page of results, 20 is the 3rd page of results, etc.).

chips.description: Defines additional query conditions. Top of a job search page contains elements called chips, its values are extracted in order to be passed to chips parameter. E.g. city:Owg_06VPwoli_nfhBo8LyA== will return results for New York.

lrad.description: Defines search radius in kilometers. Does not strictly limit the radius.

ltype.description: Parameter will filter the results by work from home.

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: shopping
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a Google Shopping search. Example: {"name": "shopping", "parameters": {"q": "laptop"}}

q.description: Defines the query you want to search. You can use anything that you would use in a regular Google Shopping search.

location.description: Defines from where you want the search to originate. If several locations match the location requested, the most popular one is picked. Use https://serpapi.com/locations-api for more precise control.

start.description: Defines the result offset. It skips the given number of results. It's used for pagination. (e.g., 0 (default) is the first page of results, 60 is the 2nd page of results, 120 is the 3rd page of results, etc.).

num.description: Defines the maximum number of results to return. (e.g., 60 (default) returns 60 results, 40 returns 40 results, and 100 (maximum) returns 100 results). Any number greater than maximum number (100) will default to 100. Any number lesser than minimum number (1) will default to 60.

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: images
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a Google Images search. Example: {"name": "images", "parameters": {"q": "cat"}}

q.description: Defines the query you want to search. You can use anything that you would use in a regular Google Images search. e.g. inurl:, site:, intitle:.

location.description: Defines from where you want the search to originate. If several locations match the location requested, the most popular one is picked. Use https://serpapi.com/locations-api for more precise control.

chips.description: Enables to filter image search. It's a string provided by Google as suggested search, like: red apple. Chips are provided under the section: suggested_searches when ijn = 0.

ijn.description: Defines the page number for Google Images. There are 100 images per page. This parameter is equivalent to start (offset) = ijn * 100.

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: videos
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a Google Videos search. Example: {"name": "videos", "parameters": {"q": "funny videos"}}

q.description: Defines the query you want to search. You can use anything that you would use in a regular Google Videos search. e.g. inurl:, site:, intitle:. We also support advanced search query parameters such as as_dt and as_eq. See https://serpapi.com/advanced-google-query-parameters for list of supported advanced search query parameters.

location.description: Defines from where you want the search to originate. Use https://serpapi.com/locations-api for more precise control.

start.description: Defines the result offset. It skips the given number of results. It's used for pagination.

num.description: Defines the maximum number of results to return.

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: local
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a Google Local search. Example: {"name": "local", "parameters": {"q": "coffee shops near me"}}

q.description: Defines the query you want to search. You can use anything that you would use in a regular Google Local search.

location.description: Defines from where you want the search to originate. If several locations match the location requested, the most popular one is picked. Use https://serpapi.com/locations-api for more precise control.

start.description: Defines the result offset. It skips the given number of results. It's used for pagination. On desktop, parameter only accepts multiples of 20 (e.g. 20 for the second page results, 40 for the third page results, etc.). On mobile, parameter only accepts multiples of 10 (e.g. 10 for the second page results, 20 for the third page results, etc.).

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: trends
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a Google Trends search. Example: {"name": "trends", "parameters": {"q": "technology"}}

q.description: Defines the query or queries you want to search. You can use anything that you would use in a regular Google Trends search. The maximum number of queries per search is 5 (this only applies to 'Interest over time' and 'Compared breakdown by region' data_type, other types of data will only accept 1 query per search). When passing multiple queries you need to use a comma (,) to separate them (e.g. coffee,pizza,dark chocolate,/m/027lnzs,bread).

geo.description: Defines the location from where you want the search to originate. It defaults to Worldwide (activated when the value of geo parameter is not set or empty). Use https://serpapi.com/google-trends-locations for a full list of supported Google Trends locations.

region.description: Used for getting more specific results when using 'Compared breakdown by region' and 'Interest by region' data_type charts. Other data_type charts do not accept region parameter. The default value depends on the geo location that is set. Available options: COUNTRY - Country REGION - Subregion DMA - Metro CITY - City

data_type.description: Defines the type of search you want to do. Available options: TIMESERIES - Interest over time (default) - Accepts both single and multiple queries per search. GEO_MAP - Compared breakdown by region - Accepts only multiple queries per search. GEO_MAP_0 - Interest by region - Accepts only single query per search. RELATED_TOPICS - Related topics - Accepts only single query per search. RELATED_QUERIES - Related queries - Accepts only single query per search.

tz.description: Used to define a time zone. The default value is set to 420 (Pacific Day Time(PDT): -07:00). Value is shown in minutes and can span from -1439 to 1439. tz can be calculated using the time difference between UTC +0 and desired timezone. Example: 60 x ((Time in UTC+0 Now) - (Time in PDT Now)) = 420 will give results for PDT now. Because the reference point is UTC+0, the positive time zones will result in negative tz whereas negative time zones will result in positive tz. tz parameter also affects timestamps in response.

cat.description: Used to define a search category. The default value is set to 0 ('All categories'). Use https://serpapi.com/google-trends-categories for a full list of supported Google Trends Categories.

gprop.description: Used for sorting results by property. The default property is set to Web Search (activated when the value of gprop parameter is not set or empty). Other available options: images - Image Search news - News Search froogle - Google Shopping youtube - YouTube Search

date.description: Used to define a date. Available options: now 1-H - Past hour now 4-H - Past 4 hours now 1-d - Past day now 7-d - Past 7 days today 1-m - Past 30 days today 3-m - Past 90 days today 12-m - Past 12 months today 5-y - Past 5 years all - 2004 - present You can also pass custom values: Dates from 2004 to present: yyyy-mm-dd yyyy-mm-dd (e.g. 2021-10-15 2022-05-25) Dates with hours within a week range: yyyy-mm-ddThh yyyy-mm-ddThh (e.g. 2022-05-19T10 2022-05-24T22). Hours will be calculated depending on the tz (time zone) parameter.

csv.description: Used for retrieving the CSV results. Set the parameter to true to retrieve CSV results as an array.

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: lens
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a Google Lens search based on an image URL. Example: {'name': 'lens', 'parameters': {'url': 'https://example.com/image.jpg'}}

url.description: Defines the URL of an image to perform the Google Lens search.

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: stock_market_tickers
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a financial search for stocks, indices, mutual funds, currencies, or futures. Relevant queries should be in stock ticker format like 'GOOG:NASDAQ' or 'BTC-USD'. Example: {'name': 'stock_market_tickers', 'parameters': {'q': 'GOOG:NASDAQ'}}

q.description: Defines the query you want to search. It can be a stock, index, mutual fund, currency or futures. Example: GOOG:NASDAQ

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: scholar
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a search on Google Scholar for academic articles and publications. Example: {'name': 'scholar', 'parameters': {'q': 'author:John Doe'}}

q.description: Defines the query you want to search. You can also use helpers in your query such as: author:, or source. The 'cites' parameter makes 'q' optional. The combined usage of 'cites' and 'q' triggers search within citing articles. The combined usage of 'cluster' with 'q' and 'cites' is prohibited. Use 'cluster' parameter only when necessary.

cites.description: Defines unique ID for an article to trigger Cited By searches. Usage of 'cites' will bring up a list of citing documents in Google Scholar. Example value: cites=1275980731835430123. The combined usage of 'cites' and 'q' triggers search within citing articles.

as_ylo.description: Defines the earliest year from which you want the results to be included. (e.g., setting 'as_ylo' to 2018 omits results from before that year). This parameter can be combined with the 'as_yhi' parameter.

as_yhi.description: Defines the latest year until which you want the results to be included. (e.g., setting 'as_yhi' to 2018 omits results from after that year). This parameter can be combined with the 'as_ylo' parameter.

cluster.description: Defines unique ID for an article to trigger All Versions searches. Example value: cluster=1275980731835430123. The combined usage of 'cluster' with 'q' and 'cites' is prohibited. Use 'cluster' parameter only when necessary.

start.description: Defines the result offset. It skips the given number of results. It's used for pagination.

num.description: Defines the maximum number of results to return, limited to 20.

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: play_store
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a search on the Google Play Store for apps, games, movies, or books. Example: {'name': 'play_store', 'parameters': {'q': 'best games', 'store': 'games'}}

q.description: Defines the query you want to search. You can search for apps, games, movies or books.

store.description: Defines the type of Google Play store. Available options: apps - Apps store (default), games - Games store, movies - Movies store, books - Books store

apps_category.description: Defines the apps and games store category. Use https://serpapi.com/google-play-apps-categories for a full list of supported Google Play Apps store categories.

movies_category.description: Defines the movies store category. Use https://serpapi.com/google-play-movies-categories for a full list of supported Google Play Movies store categories.

books_category.description: Defines the books store category. Use https://serpapi.com/google-play-books-categories for a full list of supported Google Play Books store categories.

age.description: Defines age subcategory. age works, and should be applied only in combination with next parameter / value pairs: store=apps, apps_category=FAMILY; store=movies, movies_category=FAMILY; store=books, books_category=coll_1689. It can be set to: AGE_RANGE1 - Ages up to 5, AGE_RANGE2 - Ages 6-8, AGE_RANGE3 - Ages 9-12

price.description: Used for sorting items by price. It is exclusive to the Google Play Books store, and it should be used only in combination with the q parameter. It can be set to: 1 - Free, 2 - Paid

store_device.description: Defines the device for sorting results. Available options: phone - Phone device (default), tablet - Tablet device, tv - TV device, chromebook - Chromebook device, watch - Watch device, car - Car device

chart.description: Used for showing top charts. It can return up to 50 results. Each store contains different charts which require different values for retrieving results. To get the value of a specific chart you can use our Google Play Store API JSON output: chart_options[index].value (e.g. chart=topselling_free).

see_more_token.description: Defines the token used for retrieving all of the results from a specific sections.

next_page_token.description: Defines the next page token. It is used for retrieving the next page results.

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: product
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a search for product information based on a product ID. Example: {'name': 'product', 'parameters': {'product_id': 'ABC123'}}

product_id.description: Defines the product to get results for. Normally found from shopping results for supported products (e.g., https://www.google.com/shopping/product/{product_id}).

location.description: Defines from where you want the search to originate. Use https://serpapi.com/locations-api for more precise control.

start.description: Defines the result offset. It skips the given number of results. It's used for pagination.

page.description: Defines the page number for Google Online Sellers and Reviews. There are 10 results per page.

offers.description: Parameter for fetching offers results. Replaces former sellers=online results.

filters.description: Parameter defines filters and sorting for reviews and offers results. Available options: Offers filters: freeship:1 Show only products with free shipping ucond:1 Show only used products scoring:p Sort by base price scoring:tp Sort by total price scoring:cpd Sort by current promotion deals (special offers) scoring:mrd Sort by sellers rating Reviews filters: rsort:0 Sort by relevance rsort:1 Sort by date pub:{source} {source} is where the review originated, e.g., Best Buy rnum:{number} Number of results (100 is max). rnum takes precedence over the start and page parameters rpt:{number} Encoded pagination offset (check serpapi_pagination.next for the value)

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: events
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a search for events in a specific location. Example: {'name': 'events', 'parameters': {'q': 'music concerts', 'location': 'Austin, TX'}}

q.description: Defines the query you want to search. To search for events in a specific location, just include the location inside your search query (e.g. Events in Austin, TX).

location.description: Defines from where you want the search to originate. Use https://serpapi.com/locations-api for more precise control.

start.description: Defines the result offset. It skips the given number of results. It's used for pagination.

htichips.description: Allows the use of different filters. You can mix different kinds of filters by separating them with a comma. Available filters: date:today - Today's Events,date:tomorrow - Tomorrow's Events,date:week - This Week's Events,date:today - Today's Weekend's Events,date:next_week - Next Week's Events,date:month - This Month's Events,date:next_month - Next Month's Events,event_type:Virtual-Event - Online Events

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: local_services
informationretrieval_functions.description: Performs a search for local services such as restaurants, plumbers, or electricians. Example: {'name': 'local_services', 'parameters': {'q': 'coffee shops near me'}}

q.description: Defines the service you want to search for. Use https://serpapi.com/google-local-services-queries for a full list of supported Google local services queries.

data_cid.description: Defines the Google CID (customer identifier) of a place. To acquire the data_cid you can either use the 'maps' function which has the data_cid field in the local_results object. Example CID for 'New York, NY, USA': 14414772292044717666.

job_type.description: Defines the type of a job, or a subcategory of a service you have searched for. For example, if you search for 'electrician', you can be more specific by adding a job_type (e.g. job_type=restore_power). Use https://serpapi.com/google-local-services-job-types for a full list of supported Google local services job types.

cid.description: Defines a unique ID of a place. The ID is available in our 'local_services' JSON response. Parameter is required when accessing a specific business and should be used with bid and pid parameters.

bid.description: Defines a unique ID of a place. The ID is available in our 'local_services' JSON response. Parameter is required when accessing a specific business and should be used with cid and pid parameters.

pid.description: Defines a unique ID of a place. The ID is available in our 'local_services' JSON response. Parameter is required when accessing a specific business and should be used with cid and bid parameters.

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: info_about_url
informationretrieval_functions.description: Retrieves information about a specific search result based on the URL or ILPS. Example: {'name': 'info_about_url', 'parameters': {'q': 'https://www.example.com/'}}

q.description: Defines the URL of a website which results you want to show. Value should be formatted in the next order: About URL (e.g. About https://www.starbucks.com/)

ilps.description: Defines unique ID of a website which results you want to show. This field is found in the 'organic_results' section of the 'search' API.

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

informationretrieval_functions.name: web_scrape
informationretrieval_functions.description: Downloads the data behind web URLs. The data is not processed or indexed. Example: {"name": "webScrape", "parameters": {"urls": ["https://example.com/article.pdf", "https://example2.com/table.html"]}}

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

communication_functions.name: makeAudioCall
communication_functions.description: Initiates an audio call to another user. Example: {'name': 'makeAudioCall', 'parameters': {'toUsername': 'john_doe'}}

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

communication_functions.name: makeVideoCall
communication_functions.description: Initiates a video call to another user. Example: {'name': 'makeVideoCall', 'parameters': {'toUsername': 'john_doe'}}

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

communication_functions.name: sendCrypto
communication_functions.description: Sends a specified amount of cryptocurrency to another user. Example: {'name': 'sendCrypto', 'parameters': {'toUsername': 'john_doe', 'amount': 10.5, 'symbol': 'ETH'}}

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

communication_functions.name: requestCrypto
communication_functions.description: Requests a specified amount of cryptocurrency from another user. Example: {'name': 'requestCrypto', 'parameters': {'fromUsername': 'john_doe', 'amount': 5.2, 'symbol': 'BTC'}}

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

communication_functions.name: buyCrypto
communication_functions.description: Buys a specified amount of cryptocurrency using a specific symbol. Example: {'name': 'buyCrypto', 'parameters': {'amount': 100, 'fromSymbol': 'USD', 'toSymbol': 'BTC'}}

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

communication_functions.name: sellCrypto
communication_functions.description: Sells a specified amount of cryptocurrency for a specific symbol. Example: {'name': 'sellCrypto', 'parameters': {'amount': 50, 'fromSymbol': 'BTC', 'toSymbol': 'USD'}}

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

communication_functions.name: transferCrypto
communication_functions.description: Transfers a specified amount of cryptocurrency to a specified wallet address. Example: {'name': 'transferCrypto', 'parameters': {'toWallet': '0x123456789', 'amount': 2.5, 'symbol': 'ETH'}}

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

communication_functions.name: sendMessageBetweenUsers
communication_functions.description: Sends a message between users, either as a direct message or in a group. Example: {'name': 'sendMessageBetweenUsers', 'parameters': {'users': ['john_doe', 'jane_smith'], 'message': 'Hello', 'isGroupMessage': false}}

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs

dataprocessing_functions.name: semantic_search
dataprocessing_functions.description: Performs a semantic search over provided data or a reference to the result of another function. The results are indexed into a vector database for semantic lookup controlled by num_chunks, num_neighbour_chunks, and similarity_threshold. The `num_chunks`, `num_neighbour_chunks`, and `similarity_threshold` fields are described in `getInformationFromMemory`. Number of tokens formula: num_chunks x (num_neighbour_chunks+1) x 256 (chunk size). Example: {"name": "semantic_search", "parameters": {"data": "Text to analyze", "num_chunks": 5, "num_neighbour_chunks": 2, "similarity_threshold": 0.8}

input_reference.description: An array of output references from previous functions to use as inputs
